claim: Surfaced roughly $800K in at-risk revenue across hundreds of thousands of transactions at a heavy-duty industrial parts manufacturer; the recovery plan was adopted by company leadership.

variants.long: Built the transaction-level analytics across hundreds of thousands of records at a heavy-duty industrial parts manufacturer that surfaced roughly $800K in at-risk revenue, and turned it into a recovery plan leadership adopted.

claim: Mapped hundreds of customer buying-group transitions and identified seven figures in recoverable revenue from group-driven churn.

variants.short: Mapped buying-group transitions; seven figures recoverable.
variants.long: Traced distributor buying-group transitions to quantify which moves drove churn, isolating seven figures in recoverable revenue and a target campaign list.

variants.short: Showed most lapsed accounts still buying from competitors.
variants.long: Worked roughly 1,600 accounts the business had written off, proving most were still operating and purchasing from competitors, which reframed the dead book as an addressable recovery opportunity.

variants.long: Engineered product-level displacement features across hundreds of accounts that flag category exit months ahead, turning slow purchasing drift into an actionable early-warning.

claim: Shipped a production churn model (XGBoost, strong measured AUC) with Random Survival Forest survival timing and per-cluster IsolationForest anomaly detection, validated by temporal cross-validation.

variants.short: Shipped production churn ML (XGBoost) + survival + anomaly models.
variants.long: Designed and shipped the production ML stack for a B2B revenue-intelligence platform: an XGBoost churn classifier with strong measured AUC, a Random Survival Forest for timing, and per-cluster IsolationForest anomaly detection, all validated under temporal cross-validation rather than random splits.

claim: Designed and shipped production LLM-agent systems end to end: an orchestrator + parallel sub-agent framework with deterministic guardrails, MCP tool servers, and multi-model routing.

variants.short: Built production LLM-agent systems (orchestrator + guardrails + MCP).
variants.long: Built agentic systems that run unattended in production: an orchestrator dispatching parallel sub-agents, deterministic guardrails and verification gates wrapping every LLM call, MCP tool servers, and cost-aware multi-model routing.

proof: E:Alice scripts/ harness + Cadence Analytics agent layer

claim: AI-native operator-builder: carries work from customer signal to shipped system, building and shipping production ML and agentic systems with the discipline to make them trustworthy.

variants.short: AI-native operator-builder (ships production ML + agents).
variants.long: AI-native in the literal sense — builds and runs production ML and agentic systems, with the architectural discipline (guardrails, verification, observability) to make them trustworthy, rather than just prompting a chatbot.

claim: The rare combination as ONE capability: an operator who carries enterprise GTM outcomes (a major aerospace OEM at Lattice Additive) AND ships production ML and agents (Cadence Analytics, Alice) — the same person carries it from customer signal to shipped system, with no handoff.

variants.short: Carries enterprise GTM outcomes AND ships production ML/agents — one person.
variants.long: The differentiator is the intersection, not either half: someone who has owned enterprise customer outcomes for a major aerospace OEM and ships production ML and agentic systems. The value is that one person carries it from customer signal to shipped system, with no handoff.

claim: Two production agent systems designed, built, and run: Cadence Analytics (B2B revenue-intelligence platform) and Alice (autonomous job-search agent with safety guardrails and LLMOps observability).

variants.short: Two production agent systems designed, built, and run (Cadence Analytics, Alice).
variants.long: Two distinct agent systems running in production, designed and built end to end: Cadence Analytics' revenue-intelligence platform and Alice, an autonomous job-search agent with real safety guardrails and observability. Both run unattended, which is what trustworthy autonomous-agent engineering has to clear.

claim: Self-taught and non-credentialed (advanced-manufacturing technical background), shipping production ML and full-stack software — proof of learning velocity.

variants.short: Self-taught, non-CS, shipping production ML + full-stack.
variants.long: Came from an advanced-manufacturing technical background with no CS credential and self-taught into shipping production ML and full-stack systems — the relevant signal is learning velocity and the discipline to make AI systems trustworthy.

claim: Built custom multi-pass LLM-agent orchestration with no framework, re-implemented across three transports (agent SDK, raw httpx/OpenAI-shape, raw Anthropic HTTP) with manual prompt-cache management, MCP tool servers, PreToolUse allowlist hooks, and mid-session model routing.

variants.short: Custom agent orchestration, no framework, across 3 transports.
variants.long: Engineered agent orchestration from first principles rather than reaching for a framework: a multi-pass orchestrator with per-step state, MCP tool servers, PreToolUse allowlist hooks, and cost-aware mid-session model routing, then proved it transport-independent by re-implementing it three ways (agent SDK, raw OpenAI-shape httpx, raw Anthropic HTTP).

claim: Built trustworthy-agent infrastructure: fail-loud runtime guardrails, a fresh-checkout pre-push integrity gate (AST analysis + pytest on a clean tree), and Phoenix/OpenTelemetry manual spans on a raw-HTTP LLM client — instrumenting what auto-instrumentation cannot reach.

variants.short: Built trustworthy-agent infra: guardrails, integrity gate, LLM tracing.
variants.long: The differentiated 'safe autonomous agent' story: fail-loud runtime guards that block forbidden actions, a pre-push integrity gate that AST-walks a fresh checkout to catch commits missing their own dependencies, and hand-written Phoenix/OTel spans wrapping a raw-HTTP LLM client so every call's model, tokens, and cost are observable.

claim: Hand-ported an XGBoost model to a dependency-free TypeScript tree-walker so it runs on serverless edge (Vercel) with no Python or ONNX runtime.

variants.short: Ported XGBoost to dependency-free TS for serverless edge inference.
variants.long: Shipped ML to the edge without dragging a Python runtime along: hand-wrote a zero-native-dependency TypeScript tree-walker that evaluates the trained XGBoost model directly, so inference runs inside a serverless function with no Python or ONNX runtime to deploy.

claim: Applied ML methodology rigor most practitioners skip: a standalone leakage-diagnostics workflow (correlation thresholds, chi-square, ablation AUC-drop), 12-window temporal cross-validation, isotonic calibration, and strong survival concordance.

variants.short: ML rigor: leakage diagnostics, temporal CV, calibration.
variants.long: Treated validity as a first-class problem: a dedicated leakage-diagnostics module (correlation/chi-square/ablation), 12-window temporal cross-validation instead of random splits, isotonic probability calibration, and a strong-concordance survival model. The leakage discipline is the standout most ML work omits.

claim: Built a hybrid retrieval pipeline: BM25 plus MiniLM dense embeddings fused with Reciprocal Rank Fusion, an LLM-mediated final selection, and a self-built WER evaluation harness.

variants.short: Hybrid BM25 + dense retrieval (RRF) with LLM-mediated selection.
variants.long: Built retrieval that combines lexical and semantic signal: BM25 unioned with local MiniLM dense embeddings, fused via Reciprocal Rank Fusion, with an LLM-mediated final pick and a self-written word-error-rate harness to measure the upstream ASR step.

claim: Built a multi-module SKU resolution engine that translates free-form rep and voice input into canonical manufacturer part numbers over a ~10K-active-SKU ERP catalog: a 300+-pattern regex grammar (invertible), a custom Levenshtein 4-tier matching cascade, an in-memory inverted-index catalog, a calibrated confidence gradient, a 'never invent a SKU' guarantee, and a full integration-test suite. Standard-library only, no ML or LLM.

variants.short: Built a multi-module SKU resolution engine over a ~10K-SKU ERP catalog.
variants.long: Solved entity resolution at scale the hard way: a 300+-pattern invertible regex grammar that decodes part-number structure, a hand-written Levenshtein 4-tier cascade (exact to normalized-edit-distance) over family-prefix-bucketed candidates, inverted indexes behind a swappable catalog protocol, and rule-based disambiguation with a calibrated confidence gradient and a hard 'never invent a SKU' guarantee. Standard-library only.

claim: Built a rules-based voice/free-text normalizer: NATO-phonetic decoding, spoken-number and fraction parsing, and unit handling — no embeddings.

variants.short: Built a rules-based voice/free-text normalizer (NATO-phonetic, spoken numbers).
variants.long: Wrote the front end that turns spoken or typed shorthand into structured input: NATO-phonetic decoding, spoken-number and fraction parsing, and unit normalization, all rules-based rather than learned, so it is deterministic and debuggable.

claim: Engineering discipline under test: a large pytest suite (agent repo plus catalog-fixture integration tests) plus Vitest, a dual Node+Python GitHub Actions CI gate, and a pre-push integrity check.

variants.short: Real test + CI discipline: large pytest suite, dual-language CI gate.
variants.long: Ships with the rigor of a team: a large pytest suite (including safety-invariant tests and catalog-fixture integration tests), Vitest on the TypeScript side, a GitHub Actions CI that gates every change on both tsc and the Python suite, and a pre-push integrity gate.

claim: Built click-to-call telephony into the platform: Cloudflare Workers webhook ingestion, Twilio dialing with native caller-ID validation, and a unified event store.

variants.short: Built click-to-call telephony (Cloudflare Workers + Twilio + event store).
variants.long: Added a calling layer to the platform end to end: Cloudflare Workers ingesting inbound webhooks, Twilio click-to-call with native caller-ID validation, and a unified event store tying call activity back to the account record.
